pick.rationale: In this blockbuster Sweet 16 matchup, Houston's elite defense clashes with Illinois's top-ranked offense. The Cougars rank inside the top five nationally in both KenPom and Torvik, anchored by a suffocating defensive unit that ranks fourth in adjusted efficiency. While Illinois boasts the most efficient scoring attack in the country, their 24th-ranked defense leaves them vulnerable against a balanced Houston team that also features a top-15 offense. In a game where possessions will be fiercely contested, Houston's physical style of play and superior overall efficiency margin give them the edge to slow down the Fighting Illini and advance to the Elite Eight.

reasoningStep.title: Analyze Illinois vs Houston Matchup
reasoningStep.summary: Compared the statistical profiles of Illinois and Houston, noting Houston's slight edge in overall efficiency and elite defense against Illinois's top-ranked offense.
evidence: Houston ranks 5th in both KenPom and Torvik with a 33.55 AdjEM.
Illinois ranks 7th in KenPom and 6th in Torvik with a 32.14 AdjEM.
Houston features the 4th-ranked adjusted defense and 16th-ranked adjusted offense.
Illinois features the 1st-ranked adjusted offense but a 24th-ranked adjusted defense.
Houston's physical defense is well-equipped to challenge Illinois's elite scoring attack.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
